summary: Map the implementation surface for Co-op Phase 2 WorldDamageToPlayer (damage write-sites, player-body apply API, existing coop infra, wiring/idempotency)

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.gameObject.CompareTag("Body") -> GetComponent<BodyScript>(); HAS isPlayer discriminator (player branch at 5201 requires component.isPlayer && sqrMag>67). Host CAN tell it is a player body. To tell it is BOB (not host's own player): component.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. Player-branch values: health -= relVel*1.5*bodyDamageMult, stamina -= relVel*2*bodyDamageMult, shockTime += relVel*0.045*bodyDamageMult.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Prefix snapshots health/stamina/shockTime/controlState on the puppet BodyScript; Postfix diffs and emits PlayerWorldDamage(CrateImpact) if RemoteMarker(Player) present and IsHost.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: On the client the LOCAL Bob body has NO RemoteMarker so this path would double-apply. Prefix returns false / skips the player-damage block when target is the local co-op player on a non-host client (coop active && !IsHost).
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Harmony Prefix+Postfix on CrateScript.OnCollisionEnter2D. Host detect+emit; client suppress local-player block; owner applies delta + EnterHalfControl(). Cosmetic on ghost (RemotePlayerController overwrites health next frame).

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.gameObject.layer==7 -> TryGetComponent<LimbScript>() -> component2.body. HAS isPlayer discriminator (5241). Bob-vs-host: component2.body.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Same OnCollisionEnter2D patch (one method) — delta-snapshot on component2.body identically.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Same patch; suppress player limb-branch when target body is local co-op player on non-host client.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Folded into the single CrateScript.OnCollisionEnter2D patch; both branches resolve to one BodyScript — snapshot/diff that body once.

damageSources.signature: public static void CreateExplosion(GameObject explosionObj, Vector2 pos, float range, float force, float damage, int fireAmount, AudioClip sound)
damageSources.targetIdentification: Physics2D.OverlapCircleAll(pos,range); TryGetComponent<LimbScript>() (6273) else TryGetComponent<BodyScript>() (6307). NO isPlayer check — applies to ANY body/limb. Host CANNOT tell it is Bob from this method; MUST use component3.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. Impulse (6266-6270) hits EVERY rigidbody in range before type checks.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Pre-scan OverlapCircleAll for puppet bodies, snapshot health in a Prefix; Postfix diffs and emits PlayerWorldDamage(Explosion) per puppet. Impulse re-computed at owner from normalize(puppetPos-pos)*force*mass (a force, not a readable field delta).
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Per spec §8 the client does NOT run damaging CreateExplosion — it plays cosmetic ExplosionFx(pos,range) only; grenade/rocket are host-owned/suppressed. Real damage+impulse to local Bob arrives via channel.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Host-only vanilla CreateExplosion + puppet pre/post snapshot -> PlayerWorldDamage(dHealth, impulse, control=HalfControl). Client: ExplosionFx cosmetic only.

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.GetComponent<LimbScript>() (!dismembered) -> .body (7623) AND collision.GetComponent<BodyScript>() (7630). NO isPlayer check. Host MUST use GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. Discrete (Enter only).
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Prefix KillZone.OnTriggerEnter2D: if target is puppet (RemoteMarker Player) && IsHost, emit PlayerWorldDamage(KillZone, dHealth=-9999, control=NoControl) and skip puppet mutation (return false).
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: suppress vanilla kill on local player body; death arrives via channel (idempotent by EventSeq since Enter can re-fire).
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Prefix KillZone.OnTriggerEnter2D. Host emit + skip; client suppress; owner: health<=0 -> Damaged() -> Death() (no marker, DeathPatch allows).

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.gameObject.GetComponent<BodyScript>() (7601) OR GetComponent<LimbScript>().body (7607). NO isPlayer check. Host MUST use GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. CONTINUOUS (every FixedUpdate).
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Postfix accumulates dHealth/dStamina into a per-(playerId,source=Incinerator) accumulator; a ~15 Hz flusher emits a coalesced PlayerWorldDamage UNRELIABLE.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: suppress per-frame health/stamina writes on local player; coalesced drain arrives via channel.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Coalescing accumulator (shared with fire/water/oxygen). Source=Incinerator. Unreliable coalesced flush; suppress vanilla on client local player.

damageSources.source: saw (discrete DoHit; OnCollisionStay SendMessage is a no-op on bodies)
damageSources.targetIdentification: DoHit receives LimbScript -> limb.body; caller OnCollisionEnter2D resolves body via GetClosestLimb or direct LimbScript (16419-16425), 0.3s cool throttle. NO isPlayer check. Host MUST use limb.body.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. OnCollisionStay2D SendMessage("Damage",...) (16432) is a no-op on bodies (BodyScript has no Damage(float)); real player damage is ONLY in DoHit.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Prefix+Postfix on SawScript.DoHit: snapshot limb.body health/stamina/shockTime/controlState; Postfix diffs -> PlayerWorldDamage(Saw, dHealth, dStamina, dShock, control=HalfControl, impulse=knockBack dir). 0.3s-throttled, discrete.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: Prefix SawScript.DoHit returns false when limb.body is local co-op player; channel delivers.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Patch DoHit only (Stay SendMessage no-op). Reliable+deduped (EventSeq). Owner applies bundle + EnterHalfControl + knockback impulse.

damageSources.targetIdentification: Physics2D.OverlapCircleAll(transform.position,1f) (6604); TryGetComponent<BodyScript>() guarded by !isRobot && !fireImmune && fireImmunityTime<0 (6611). NO isPlayer check. Host MUST use component.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. Continuous (0.3s tick). 6610 SendMessage("Damage") is the crate/glass path; real player damage is 6613-6614 direct writes.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Postfix accumulates dHealth/dStamina (+burnIntensity) per-(playerId,Fire) accumulator, flush ~15 Hz UNRELIABLE. On threshold cross (stamina<maxHealth*0.5) include dShock(to 1f)+control=HalfControl in that flush.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: suppress direct health/stamina/burnIntensity/shockTime writes on local player; cosmetic fire particle stays local.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Coalescing accumulator, Source=Fire. Suppress vanilla writes on client local player.

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.TryGetComponent<LimbScript>() -> component.body (18384). NO isPlayer check. Host MUST use component.body.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. Only when damagePerSecond>0 (most water is buoyancy-only).
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Postfix accumulates dHealth into per-(playerId,Water) accumulator, flush ~15 Hz UNRELIABLE. Buoyancy/splash is cosmetic, owner-local, not in channel.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: suppress the health write for local player when damagePerSecond>0; buoyancy stays local (owner physics).
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Coalescing accumulator, Source=Water, active only when damagePerSecond>0. Suppress vanilla health write on client local player.

writesFields: forcedOxyLoss (=30 byte flag; downstream health/stamina loss in BodyScript.FixedUpdate ~3679-3687)
damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.TryGetComponent<BodyScript>() + critical-limb path. NO isPlayer check. Sets a FLAG, not health directly — actual damage is computed later from the body's own oxygen sim.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: AMBIGUOUS. Real damage runs in BodyScript.FixedUpdate from owner-authoritative oxygen; the puppet's oxygen sim on host is meaningless. Zone is static geometry present on BOTH machines, so Bob's local body self-drowns correctly. Host detection likely unnecessary.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Likely NONE — owner-local oxygen sim is already correct (same scene/zone both machines). Treat like fall damage.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: FLAG / default EXCLUDE (owner-local like fall damage). Verify in-game; route via channel only if puppet forcedOxyLoss leaks into owner state (it does not — owner overwrites health).

damageSources.targetIdentification: collision.GetComponent<BodyScript>() to detect, AddForce to attachedRigidbody. NO isPlayer check. Pure physics force, no field writes. Host could use GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: AMBIGUOUS. Static zone present on both machines — Bob's local body is pushed locally and correctly (owner-authoritative physics). Pushing the puppet on host is cosmetic, overwritten by owner velocity.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: NONE if zone is static and present on client — owner physics correct.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Spec §9 marks PushZone long-tail. Default EXCLUDE — owner-local physics on shared static zone is correct. Include only as host-triggered 'push-as-effect' (Activate). FLAG.

damageSources.targetIdentification: Physics2D.OverlapBoxAll on zone collider; TryGetComponent<BodyScript>()/LimbScript. NO isPlayer check. Activate is host-only per §7. Host uses GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player to know it is Bob's puppet.
damageSources.hostDetectPoint: Per §7 triggers/Activate run HOST-ONLY. When TeleportZone.Activate moves Bob's puppet, host emits PlayerWorldDamage(Teleport) carrying a NEW absolute position (dHealth/dStamina/dShock=0). Needs position payload not in base bundle — see open question.
damageSources.clientSuppressPoint: Non-host client: local triggers/Activate fully suppressed (§7); owner moves real body only on channel receipt.
damageSources.recommendedApproach: Host-only Activate; emit PlayerWorldDamage(Teleport, targetPos). Client suppresses local Activate. Position does not fit dHealth/dShock bundle — reuse impulse Vec2 as absolute target or add posOverride (open question).

excludeFromChannel.reason: Fall damage is owner-local physics; the real Bob falls and computes his own fall damage correctly on his own machine. Spec §3.2 explicitly excludes it — including it would double-apply.
Second fall-damage entry (limb landing). Owner-local; limb collisions are not replicated. Excluded for the same reason.

Writes only forcedOxyLoss flag; actual health/stamina loss runs in BodyScript.FixedUpdate from the body's own oxygen sim. Zone is static geometry present on both machines, so Bob's local body self-drowns correctly (owner-local, like fall damage). Default exclude; verify in-game.

Pure AddForce on a static trigger zone present on both machines; owner-authoritative physics already pushes Bob's local body correctly. Spec §9 marks PushZone long-tail. Exclude unless push becomes a host-triggered Activate effect.
Buoyancy is owner-local physics + cosmetic splash/sound; only the optional damagePerSecond>0 health drain belongs in the channel. Buoyancy force and splash effects are excluded.
Modifies GLOBAL Physics2D.gravity/Physics.gravity, not a per-player field. Belongs to the ActivateObject global-effect channel (spec §7), not WorldDamageToPlayer.

excludeFromChannel.path: Host's OWN player body (PlayerScript.player.bodyScript, no RemoteMarker)
excludeFromChannel.reason: Host owns both world and own body; world damage to the host applies locally via vanilla code. Spec §3.2: host body is not in this channel. Discriminator: absence of RemoteMarker on host side.

excludeFromChannel.path: CrateScript.Damage / GlassScript.Damage / DroneScript.Damage (world-entity health, decomp 5012-5094 etc.)
excludeFromChannel.reason: These damage the WORLD ENTITY (crate/glass/drone health), not a player. Already handled by Phase 1 WorldDamagePatch + DestroyWorldEntity channel. Not player damage.

World damage to enemies is host-authoritative and replicated via EnemyState/EnemyDied, not the player channel. Discriminator: target is an enemy (EntityTag / RemoteMarker.Kind==Enemy), not a player puppet.

fields.name: PlayerId
fields.purpose: Target player whose REAL body the owner must damage. On host = the puppet's RemoteMarker.PlayerId; applied only by the machine where LocalPlayerId==PlayerId.

fields.name: EventSeq
fields.purpose: Idempotency + ordering key. With Source dedups reliable discrete events via OnceGate; for coalesced streams it is advisory ordering only.

fields.name: Source
fields.purpose: CrateImpact=1, Explosion=2, KillZone=3, Incinerator=4, Saw=5, Fire=6, Water=7, Teleport=8 (Push reserved). Drives owner-side cosmetic/sound and the dedup namespace.

fields.name: DHealth
fields.purpose: body.health += DHealth (negative for damage; killzone large negative). Owner applies to real BodyScript.

fields.name: DShock
fields.purpose: body.shockTime += DShock (or driven to max when control forces it, e.g. fire sets 1f).

fields.name: Control
fields.purpose: Desired control transition on the owner body: 255 = no-change sentinel, else FullControl/HalfControl/NoControl. Owner calls EnterHalfControl()/EnterNoControl() (NoControl / health<=0 drives Death).

fields.name: ImpulseX
fields.purpose: Linear impulse X for rb.AddForce on the owner body (explosion/saw knockback). For Teleport this pair instead carries the absolute target position (open question).

fields.name: ImpulseY
fields.purpose: Linear impulse Y (or target posY for Teleport).

newProtocol.reliability: Two modes by Source: DISCRETE one-shot (CrateImpact, Explosion, KillZone, Saw, Teleport) = RELIABLE, deduped by (Source,EventSeq) via OnceGate (mirrors EnemyDied/DestroyWorldEntity reliable+idempotent). CONTINUOUS coalesced (Incinerator, Fire, Water) = UNRELIABLE, flushed ~15 Hz from a per-(playerId,source) accumulator; loss self-corrects like the BodyState stream. Mirror SendBodyState(reliable:false) vs SendEnemyDied(reliable:true).
newProtocol.idempotencyKey: (PlayerId, Source, EventSeq). Reuse OnceGate (EntitySync.cs) keyed on a composed int e.g. (Source<<24)^EventSeq, reset on scene load. For continuous unreliable streams EventSeq is advisory (ordering), not gated.

bodyApplyApi.realBodyApply: Owner (machine where LocalPlayerId==msg.PlayerId) applies to PlayerScript.player.bodyScript (the real local body, which has NO RemoteMarker so DamagedPatch/DeathPatch allow normal logic): body.health += DHealth; body.stamina += DStamina; body.shockTime += DShock; if Control==HalfControl && body.controlState==FullControl -> body.EnterHalfControl(); if Control==NoControl -> body.EnterNoControl(); then body.Damaged() (drives shock/half-control consequence + Death() when health<=0). Impulse: body.rb.AddForce(new Vector2(ImpulseX,ImpulseY), ForceMode2D.Impulse) (optionally a limb torque for explosion feel). Order matters: write health/stamina/shock FIRST, then control transition, then Damaged() LAST (mirrors vanilla crate/kick/projectile bundle order at decomp 5186-5191, 2384-2407).
bodyApplyApi.ghostNoMutate: Host does NOT persist any field on the ghost-puppet: writes to the puppet health/stamina are overwritten next FixedUpdate by RemotePlayerController (health=s.Health line 43, stamina=99f line 44). Detect-patches snapshot-in-Prefix/diff-in-Postfix WITHOUT relying on the puppet value sticking, and ideally skip the puppet mutation block (Prefix return false when RemoteMarker(Player) present on host) so the ghost stays a pure cosmetic puppet. EnterHalfControl/EnterNoControl on the puppet is acceptable as COSMETIC (RemotePlayerController already drives control via DesiredControl/RagdollHoldPatch) but is non-authoritative.

existingInfraToReuse.name: RemoteMarker (Kind==Player, PlayerId)
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Universal puppet discriminator. Host detect: target.GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() with Kind==Player identifies Bob's ghost and yields PlayerId for the message. Most world sources have NO isPlayer/owner discriminator, so this is the only reliable target-id mechanism.

existingInfraToReuse.name: WorldDamageGate (host/client gate helper)
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Existing pattern: AllowDamage(tag)/HostMaybeDestroy + ResetBroadcastGuard already wired into CoopBehaviour.Shutdown(333)/OnSceneLoaded(355). Mirror it as a PlayerWorldDamageGate: AllowLocalPlayerDamage() returns false on non-host client for the local player body; host detects+emits. Reuse the existing Reset wiring slots.

existingInfraToReuse.name: InvulnerabilityPatch (DamagedPatch/DeathPatch)
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Already blocks Damaged/Death on the puppet (RemoteMarker present). The channel relies on this: writing health on the puppet never triggers local Damaged/Death; the owner real body (no marker) runs them normally. DeathPatch's ApplyingNetworkDeath gate is the template if cosmetic puppet death is ever needed.

existingInfraToReuse.name: OnceGate / EntityCounter
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: OnceGate for idempotent reliable PlayerWorldDamage dedup keyed on (Source,EventSeq). EntityCounter pattern for allocating monotonic EventSeq per source on host. Reset() already called on scene load.

existingInfraToReuse.name: ShotReplay.Replaying + FireRelayPatch guard pattern
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Template for an 'ApplyingWorldDamage' guard: when the owner applies PlayerWorldDamage via Damaged()/Death(), set a flag so any re-detect/re-broadcast patch skips (prevents feedback), mirroring how Replaying prevents FireSeq re-bump.

existingInfraToReuse.name: BodyStateCodec Encode/Decode convention
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Template for PlayerWorldDamageCodec: MemoryStream+BinaryWriter, PacketType byte first, fields in fixed order; Decode skips the type byte and reads in identical order. Add PlayerWorldDamageCodec.cs following this exactly.

existingInfraToReuse.name: CoopSession packet routing + encoder methods
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Add PacketType.PlayerWorldDamage to enum (Protocol.cs), a case in HandleData switch (43-64) -> PlayerWorldDamageReceived event, and an EncodePlayerWorldDamage static (138+ pattern). Bump Protocol.Version (currently 2 -> 3) since the wire format changes (OnHello rejects mismatch, line 74).

existingInfraToReuse.name: CoopBehaviour wiring (MakeSession, SendBodyState, _remotes, Shutdown/OnSceneLoaded resets)
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Subscribe PlayerWorldDamageReceived += OnPlayerWorldDamage in MakeSession (132-149); add SendPlayerWorldDamage(...) broadcaster (like SendBodyState 183); OnPlayerWorldDamage applies to local body only if s.PlayerId==LocalPlayerId. Reset OnceGate + accumulators in Shutdown(326) and OnSceneLoaded(346) beside WorldDamageGate.ResetBroadcastGuard().

existingInfraToReuse.name: RemotePlayerController owner-state overwrite
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: Line 43 body.health=s.Health and line 44 stamina=99f already make the puppet non-authoritative and self-heal any stray host-side write — so host cosmetic mutation of the ghost is automatically harmless. Confirms 'host does not mutate ghost' holds by construction.

existingInfraToReuse.name: LocalBodyBroadcaster (owner authoritative health stream)
existingInfraToReuse.howUsed: After the owner applies PlayerWorldDamage to the real body, the reduced health (field Health, line 54) propagates to everyone via the existing BodyState stream. No separate result broadcast needed — the channel only delivers the discrete event to the owner; new health flows back through presence.

patchPlan.hostBehavior: Resolve target BodyScript (Body-tag and Limb-layer branches both -> one body). If body has RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: Prefix snapshot health/stamina/shockTime/controlState; Postfix diff -> SendPlayerWorldDamage(PlayerId, EventSeq++, CrateImpact, dHealth, dStamina, dShock, control=HalfControl, impulse=0) reliable. Optionally skip puppet mutation (cosmetic ghost).
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: if target is the LOCAL co-op player body, Prefix returns false on the player-damage branch. Damage arrives via channel.

patchPlan.signature: public static void CreateExplosion(GameObject explosionObj, Vector2 pos, float range, float force, float damage, int fireAmount, AudioClip sound)
patchPlan.hostBehavior: Host runs vanilla. Pre-scan OverlapCircleAll(pos,range) for puppet bodies (RemoteMarker Player), snapshot health; Postfix: per puppet dHealth=new-old, impulse=normalize(puppetPos-pos)*force*mass (recompute); SendPlayerWorldDamage(Explosion, dHealth, control=HalfControl, impulse) reliable, one per puppet hit.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Client never runs damaging CreateExplosion: grenade/rocket host-owned/suppressed; client plays cosmetic ExplosionFx(pos,range) only (spec §8). Local Bob's explosion damage arrives via channel.

patchPlan.hostBehavior: Resolve body (BodyScript or LimbScript.body). If RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: SendPlayerWorldDamage(KillZone, dHealth=-9999, control=NoControl) reliable+deduped; skip puppet mutation (return false).
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: suppress vanilla kill on local player body; death arrives via channel.
patchPlan.ownerApply: body.health+=-9999 -> Damaged() -> Death() (no marker, DeathPatch allows).

patchPlan.hostBehavior: Body/limb -> body. If RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: accumulate dHealth/dStamina into per-(PlayerId,Incinerator) accumulator; 15 Hz flusher sends PlayerWorldDamage(Incinerator) UNRELIABLE.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: suppress per-frame health/stamina writes on local player; coalesced drain arrives via channel.

patchPlan.hostBehavior: limb.body. If RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: snapshot/diff health/stamina/shockTime/control; SendPlayerWorldDamage(Saw, dH, dS, dShock, control=HalfControl, impulse=knockBack*dir) reliable+deduped (DoHit 0.3s throttled).
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: Prefix return false when limb.body is local player; channel delivers.
patchPlan.ownerApply: EnterHalfControl(); shockTime+=dShock; rb/limb.AddForce(impulse); health+=dH; stamina+=dS; Damaged().

patchPlan.hostBehavior: Per body in OverlapCircleAll with RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: accumulate dHealth/dStamina into per-(PlayerId,Fire) accumulator; flush 15 Hz UNRELIABLE. On stamina<maxHealth*0.5 threshold cross include dShock(to 1f)+control=HalfControl.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: suppress direct health/stamina/burnIntensity/shockTime writes on local player; cosmetic fire particle stays local.
patchPlan.ownerApply: health+=dH; stamina+=dS; (shockTime + EnterHalfControl on threshold); Damaged().

patchPlan.hostBehavior: If damagePerSecond>0 and component.body has RemoteMarker(Player) && IsHost: accumulate dHealth into per-(PlayerId,Water) accumulator; flush 15 Hz UNRELIABLE.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: suppress health write on local player when damagePerSecond>0. Buoyancy stays local.

patchPlan.hostBehavior: When Activate moves a puppet (RemoteMarker Player) && IsHost: SendPlayerWorldDamage(Teleport) carrying ABSOLUTE target pos (impulse pair reused as posX/posY, dHealth=0) reliable+deduped.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Non-host client: local triggers/Activate fully suppressed (§7); owner repositions only on channel receipt.
patchPlan.ownerApply: body.rb.position / transform.position = teleportPoint (from message). Requires position-carrying interpretation — see open questions.

patchPlan.signature: static bool IsRemotePlayerPuppet(BodyScript, out int playerId) / static bool SuppressLocalPlayerWorldDamage(BodyScript target)
patchPlan.hostBehavior: Centralize host detect: RemoteMarker(Player) test + PlayerId extraction; only when IsHost && coop active.
patchPlan.clientBehavior: Centralize client suppress: non-host && coop active && target==local player -> return true (skip vanilla). Reset accumulators + OnceGate on scene/shutdown beside existing WorldDamageGate.ResetBroadcastGuard.

openQuestions: Bundle shape for Teleport/position: approved fields are (dHealth,dStamina,dShock,control,impulse) — pure deltas. Teleport needs an ABSOLUTE position. Reuse the impulse Vec2 as posX/posY when Source==Teleport, or add an explicit posOverride? Confirm with spec owner. Host-triggered push-as-effect has the same need.

OxygenlessArea: confirm in-game that Bob's local body self-drowns correctly (static zone present on both machines, downstream damage owner-local in BodyScript.FixedUpdate). If yes, exclude permanently like fall damage; if the host puppet's forcedOxyLoss matters, route via channel.
PushZoneScript: same static-zone question — is owner-local physics sufficient, or do any push zones get host-spawned/Activate-triggered (then route via channel)? Spec §9 marks it long-tail.

Coalescing flush rate/reliability for continuous sources (Incinerator/Fire/Water): proposed ~15 Hz UNRELIABLE with advisory EventSeq. Confirm acceptable vs RELIABLE-coalesced. Unreliable loss slightly undershoots damage — acceptable for trusted co-op?

Explosion impulse fidelity: owner recomputes impulse from (pos,force,mass) rather than reading the puppet's post-AddForce velocity. Acceptable? Or send explosion pos+force in the message and let the owner compute exactly like vanilla (6266-6270)?

Protocol.Version bump: adding PacketType.PlayerWorldDamage + new codec changes the wire format — Version must increment 2 -> 3 (OnHello rejects mismatch). Confirm no other in-flight protocol change collides; coordinated client rollout.

ApplyingWorldDamage guard: should the owner-apply path set a flag (mirror of ShotReplay.Replaying) so the owner's own world-damage detect-patches do not re-emit when his real body takes the hit? The owner real body has no RemoteMarker so detect skips it, but verify no loop where host is also owner of nothing.
Multi-player (N>2): channel routes per PlayerId, but current code is N=2 (single-remote peer->playerId). Confirm routing is future-proof though only N=2 is tested.

GlassScript.Damage(float,Vector3) vs generic SendMessage("Damage") (spec §10 Minor): not a player source, but confirm no world-damage source uses SendMessage("Damage") that lands on a player BodyScript (verified: saw/fire SendMessage are no-ops on bodies).
risks: Target-identification gap: KillZone, Incinerator, Saw, Fire, Water, Explosion, Teleport have NO isPlayer/owner check in vanilla — the ONLY way the host knows a hit body is Bob's ghost is GetComponentInParent<RemoteMarker>() Kind==Player. If RemoteMarker is missing/on the wrong GameObject (sanitizer puts it on transform.root, which can be Level2 for swapped NPCs — see memory note coop-enemy-transform-root-gotcha), detection silently fails and Bob takes no world damage. Crate-impact is the only source with a native isPlayer discriminator.

Continuous-source flooding: Incinerator/Fire/Water fire every FixedUpdate. Per-frame discrete messages would saturate the channel. MUST coalesce; getting accumulator keying ((playerId,source)) and flush/reset lifecycle (scene load, zone exit) right is error-prone.

Double-damage if suppression misses: the local Bob body has no RemoteMarker so EVERY vanilla world path runs on it by default. If even one client-suppress point is missed, Bob takes damage twice (local + channel). Suppression must be exhaustive and symmetric with the host emit set.

Order-of-operations on owner apply: vanilla writes health/stamina/shock THEN EnterHalfControl THEN Damaged(). Wrong order (Damaged() before health write, or double EnterHalfControl) can mis-trigger the Death threshold or double-set shockTime (Damaged sets shockTime=1f independently).

Explosion is a static multi-target loop — cannot use __instance per-target snapshot; the pre-scan/diff wrapper is fragile if OverlapCircleAll ordering or list-dedup (decomp 6275/6309) differs from the snapshot scan. Impulse cannot be captured as a discrete field delta from a physics force.

Idempotency for reliable discrete events: OnTriggerEnter2D (killzone) can re-fire on re-entry; reliable resend can duplicate. Must gate by (Source,EventSeq) via OnceGate. Coalesced streams must NOT be gated (would drop legitimate repeated drains).
Teleport/position does not fit the dHealth/dStamina/dShock bundle — overloading impulse as absolute position is a semantic hack that can mis-apply if Source dispatch is wrong.

Trusted-position assumption (spec §10): host detects world impact on the puppet whose rb.position is client-driven (RemotePlayerController 107-112). A laggy/cheating client could dodge or invite world damage. Accepted as trusted co-op, but killzone/saw timing depends on puppet position fidelity.
Protocol version bump (2->3) rejects mismatched clients at handshake; coordinated rollout required.

Feedback loop: if the owner re-enters a zone after channel-applied damage, or a detect-patch accidentally runs on the owner's own host body, the host could re-emit. The host body has no RemoteMarker (skips detect) but an ApplyingWorldDamage guard (mirror ShotReplay.Replaying) is recommended for safety.
